examples.title: Variance Analysis Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate and analyze cost variances for management control
examples.code:
// VARIANCE ANALYSIS ALGORITHM
BEGIN
 // Input data
 INPUT StandardPrice, StandardQuantity
 INPUT ActualPrice, ActualQuantity
 
 // Calculate variances
 PriceVariance = (StandardPrice - ActualPrice) × ActualQuantity
 QuantityVariance = (ActualPrice) × (StandardQuantity - ActualQuantity)
 TotalVariance = PriceVariance + QuantityVariance
 
 // Determine favorability
 IF TotalVariance > 0 THEN
 Print "Favorable Variance"
 ELSE
 Print "Unfavorable Variance"
 ENDIF
 
 // Output results
 OUTPUT PriceVariance, QuantityVariance, TotalVariance
 
 // Analysis and reporting
 IF ABS(TotalVariance) > Threshold THEN
 GenerateAlert("Significant variance detected")
 IdentifyCause(TotalVariance)
 RecommendAction()
 ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates price variance (difference due to actual vs standard price) and quantity variance (difference due to actual vs standard quantity), then determines if variance is favorable (actual < standard cost) or unfavorable.

examples.title: Budget vs Actual Comparison Process
examples.description: Process to compare budgeted figures with actual results for control
examples.code:
// BUDGETARY CONTROL PROCESS
BEGIN
 // Initialize
 Set BudgetPeriod, ControlThreshold
 Initialize VarianceReport = []
 
 // Get budget and actual data
 FOR EACH budgetItem IN BudgetData DO
 actualValue = GetActual(budgetItem.itemCode)
 budgetedValue = budgetItem.allocatedAmount
 
 // Calculate variance
 variance = actualValue - budgetedValue
 variancePercent = (variance / budgetedValue) × 100
 
 // Determine status
 IF variance > ControlThreshold THEN
 status = "REVIEW_REQUIRED"
 ELSE IF variance > 0 THEN
 status = "SLIGHTLY_OVER"
 ELSE
 status = "WITHIN_LIMIT"
 ENDIF
 
 // Add to report
 ADD TO VarianceReport: {itemCode, budgetedValue, actualValue, variance, variancePercent, status}
 ENDFOR
 
 // Generate management report
 GenerateControlReport(VarianceReport)
 
 // Take corrective action for negative variances
 FOR EACH item IN VarianceReport WHERE item.status = "REVIEW_REQUIRED" DO
 AnalyzeCauses(item)
 RecommendCorrectiveAction(item)
 ENDFOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the budgetary control process: comparing actual expenditures with budgeted amounts, calculating variances, flagging items requiring management attention, and recommending corrective actions.

examples.title: ROI Calculation for Investment Centers
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate Return on Investment for performance evaluation
examples.code:
// ROI CALCULATION ALGORITHM
BEGIN
 // Input financial data for division
 INPUT Revenue, OperatingExpenses, TotalAssets, CurrentLiabilities
 
 // Calculate Net Operating Income
 NetOperatingIncome = Revenue - OperatingExpenses
 
 // Calculate Invested Capital
 InvestedCapital = TotalAssets - CurrentLiabilities
 
 // Calculate ROI
 ROI = (NetOperatingIncome / InvestedCapital) × 100
 
 // Determine performance rating
 IF ROI >= TargetROI THEN
 Print "Meets Performance Target"
 ELSE IF ROI >= 0.8 × TargetROI THEN
 Print "Approaching Target"
 ELSE
 Print "Below Target - Needs Improvement"
 ENDIF
 
 // Calculate Residual Income (alternative metric)
 CostOfCapital = 0.10 // 10% assumed
 MinimumRequiredReturn = InvestedCapital × CostOfCapital
 ResidualIncome = NetOperatingIncome - MinimumRequiredReturn
 
 OUTPUT ROI, ResidualIncome
 
 // Investment decision
 IF ResidualIncome > 0 THEN
 Print "Value Creating Investment"
 ELSE
 Print "Value Destroying Investment"
 ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates ROI and Residual Income, two key metrics for evaluating Investment Center performance. ROI measures efficiency while Residual Income shows absolute value created beyond cost of capital.
